heading: Charles “Pete” Conrad Astronomy Awards
label: 30902 Charles “Pete” Conrad Astronomy Awards
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)30902.42 U.S.C. 16792, , .Pub. L. 109–155, title VI, § 613119 Stat. 2932Dec. 30, 2005
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)30902.42 U.S.C. 16792, , .Pub. L. 109–155, title VI, § 613119 Stat. 2932Dec. 30, 2005

children.heading: .—Short Title
children.label: a .—Short Title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Charles ‘Pete’ Conrad Astronomy Awards Act”.

children.heading: .—Amateur astronomer
children.label: 1 .—Amateur astronomer
children.text: The term “amateur astronomer” means an individual whose employer does not provide any funding, payment, or compensation to the individual for the observation of asteroids and other celestial bodies, and does not include any individual employed as a professional astronomer.

children.heading: .—Minor planet center
children.label: 2 .—Minor planet center
children.text: The term “Minor Planet Center” means the Minor Planet Center of the Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory.

children.heading: .—Near-earth asteroid
children.label: 3 .—Near-earth asteroid
children.text: The term “near-Earth asteroid” means an asteroid with a perihelion distance of less than 1.3 Astronomical Units from the Sun.

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: 4 .—Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the Charles “Pete” Conrad Astronomy Awards Program established under subsection (c).

children.heading: Charles “Pete” Conrad Astronomy Awards Program.—
children.label: c Charles “Pete” Conrad Astronomy Awards Program.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall establish the Charles “Pete” Conrad Astronomy Awards Program.

children.heading: .—Awards
children.label: 2 .—Awards
children.text: The Administrator shall make awards under the Program based on the recommendations of the Minor Planet Center.

children.heading: .—Discovery of brightest near-earth asteroid
children.label: A .—Discovery of brightest near-earth asteroid
children.text: The amateur astronomer or group of amateur astronomers who in the preceding calendar year discovered the intrinsically brightest near-Earth asteroid among the near-Earth asteroids that were discovered during that year by amateur astronomers or groups of amateur astronomers.

children.heading: .—Greatest contribution to cataloguing near-earth asteroids
children.label: B .—Greatest contribution to cataloguing near-earth asteroids
children.text: The amateur astronomer or group of amateur astronomers who made the greatest contribution to the Minor Planet Center’s mission of cataloguing near-Earth asteroids during the preceding year.

children.heading: .—Award amount
children.label: 4 .—Award amount
children.text: An award under the Program shall be in the amount of $3,000.

children.heading: .—Citizen or permanent resident
children.label: A .—Citizen or permanent resident
children.text: No individual who is not a citizen or permanent resident of the United States at the time of the individual’s discovery or contribution may receive an award under this section.

children.heading: .—Finality
children.label: B .—Finality
children.text: The decisions of the Administrator in making awards under this section are final.
